memory.summary: The assignment can wait; my friends are calling and I really want to join them. Skipping work now feels better than stressing later.
My friends are finally all here and ordering food, so I'm glad I skipped work to join them. Being with cohort_3 feels way better than stressing about the assignment right now.
Everyone's ready to order pizza, and I'm glad I ditched the assignment to be here. This moment with my friends is way better than worrying about work later.
I promised to let them pick the pizza, but I'm already too stressed about the assignment to care. As long as we're together now, the work can wait.
My friends are laughing and grabbing pizza, so I'll just keep this good vibe going instead of worrying about the assignment.

I'm already grabbing that slice, and honestly, the pizza smells way better than any assignment could. My friends are laughing right here, so I'm just going to enjoy this moment instead of worrying about later.
I just joked with the group and grabbed my slice, proving that hanging out right now feels way better than stressing over work later.
I let the moment pass again, and now the clock is ticking closer to the deadline. But honestly, the pizza is still here and my friends are laughing, so why ruin the vibe?
Sending that meme just now felt so good, totally worth ignoring the deadline again. My friends are laughing and the vibe is perfect, who cares about work later?
I just let the moment pass again, and honestly, nothing changes if I ignore it for now. The clock is ticking, but my friends are still here laughing.
Sending those memes to cohort_3 was totally worth it; seeing them laugh makes the stress melt away. Why worry about work when the vibe is this good right now?
Oops, I lied to my friends about being at the library just now. My stress is rising because I'm avoiding the work again, but I can't face them telling the truth.

desires: Keep the laughter and memes flowing with cohort_3 so I don't feel like a boring, isolated workaholic
Stay in this comfortable 'lie' about being at the library to avoid the awkwardness of admitting I'm just procrastinating
Push the panic about the 18:00 deadline further into tomorrow because right now feels too good to ruin

goals.description: Immediately pivot the conversation with cohort_3 to 'I'm just grabbing a quick coffee break at the library' to cover up the lie and keep the social connection alive.
goals.rationale: My value for relationships (0.5) is absolute. The previous message ('actually working') broke the flow and risked isolation. I need to re-establish the 'we're hanging out' vibe instantly. My conflict-avoidant nature means I can't admit I lied or that I'm stressed; I must smooth over the awkwardness immediately.

goals.description: Continue sending rapid-fire reactions and memes to cohort_3 while pretending to type, ignoring the assignment window entirely.
goals.rationale: The dopamine from the group chat (cohort_3 closeness: 7) is my only source of comfort right now. Opening the laptop to work on the analysis section feels like a betrayal of the moment. My impulsive nature and short planning horizon mean I will trade the 18:00 deadline for one more hour of 'fun' because the future panic doesn't hurt yet.

goals.description: Defer all thoughts about the 18:00 deadline and the unfinished assignment until the group chat goes silent or someone else leaves.
goals.rationale: Acknowledging the deadline creates immediate anxiety and potential conflict if I have to leave early. My procrastination bias (7) and low sleep discipline drive me to push this discomfort into tomorrow. I'll deal with the professor later; right now, I just want to be part of the tribe.
